scientific_integrity.detail: This skill produces structured tool outputs rather than empirical research findings, so scientific integrity review is not applicable.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill operates within clinical/medical data processing scope and does not generate clinical or diagnostic decision outputs.

methodological_ground.detail: No experimental methodology claims are made; the skill generates deterministic structured tool outputs without asserting scientific conclusions.

code_usability.detail: Any code produced by this skill is utility-oriented, not bioinformatics analysis code; research veto code review is not applicable.

functional_suitability.note: De-anonymization refusal now fully documented with explicit response template and technical explanation of one-way hashing; scope boundaries comprehensive

reliability.note: De-anonymization refusal path documented; pydicom import failure handled gracefully; no timeout for very large batch operations remains

performance_context.note: References directory with phi_tags.json and audit-reference.md present; SKILL.md is concise at 135 lines

agent_usability.note: De-anonymization Requests section provides explicit refusal template; Stress-Case Rules block with 5 required sections is excellent; workflow steps clear

human_usability.note: When-to-Use and When-Not-to-Use sections are clearly stated; error scenarios and recovery paths are documented for typical clinical/medical data processing use cases.

security.note: PHI hashed not stored in plain text; audit log stores only hashes; de-anonymization explicitly refused with technical explanation; no credential handling needed

maintainability.note: Well-structured class with clear separation; smoke_test.py present; references/ directory properly used

agent_specific.note: Trigger description is precise; escape hatches excellent; idempotency good; composability limited by pydicom dependency

key_strengths: De-anonymization refusal now fully documented with explicit response template, one-way hashing explanation, and institutional governance referral
Comprehensive PHI tag coverage with 50+ DICOM tags mapped to clear clear/hash/replace actions
Excellent security design: PHI values hashed not stored, audit log contains only hashes and pseudonyms
Stress-Case Rules block with 5 mandatory sections ensures consistent structured output for complex requests
Graceful degradation when pydicom is unavailable — provides manual checklist fallback instead of failing silently

recommendations.title: No timeout or progress reporting for large batch operations
recommendations.problem: Batch processing of thousands of files provides no progress indication beyond per-file print statements, and no timeout mechanism exists for very large datasets.
recommendations.root_cause: anonymize_directory() uses a simple sequential loop without progress tracking or time limits.
recommendations.fix: Add a --progress flag that shows a tqdm-style progress bar. Document in SKILL.md that very large batches (>10,000 files) should be split into smaller chunks.

recommendations.title: phi_tags.json reference file not used by script at runtime
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md references references/phi_tags.json as the source of PHI tags, but the script hardcodes PHI_TAGS as a class dictionary. The reference file is not loaded at runtime.
recommendations.root_cause: The PHI tag list was implemented as a hardcoded dict rather than loaded from the reference file.
recommendations.fix: Either load PHI_TAGS from references/phi_tags.json at initialization (enabling customization without code changes), or update SKILL.md to clarify that phi_tags.json is documentation only.
